sentence_reviews.text: Structure is discovered without task labels through density, clustering, reconstruction, or factorization.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA contents.html, Chapter 19.8 'Unsupervised Learning' (lines 523-532), including clustering and mixture models; PML book1 landing page identifies the probabilistic machine-learning text and contents links (lines 0-10, 32-40).
sentence_reviews.notes: AIMA directly supports unsupervised learning, clustering, and mixture/probabilistic structure; reconstruction/factorization are plausible but not directly shown in the inspected AIMA lines.

sentence_reviews.text: The chosen learning signal and interaction pattern may not match the deployment objective or data-generating process. Its direct dependencies include Datasets, sampling & splits, Latent variables & hidden state.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Synthesized from cited node sources plus the atlas dependency context in data/atlas.json.
sentence_reviews.notes: Limitation is reviewed as a broad synthesis/tradeoff statement for the family-level node, not as a direct quotation from one source.

sentence_reviews.text: In this atlas graph, Unsupervised learning is modeled as depending on Datasets, sampling & splits, Latent variables & hidden state and feeding downstream nodes such as Semi-supervised & weak supervision, Self-supervised learning, Causal representation learning.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: data/atlas.json canonical node and dependency graph; generated from node dependencies and downstream consumers.
sentence_reviews.notes: This is an explicit internal atlas-graph positioning claim, not an external-world necessity claim.
